conditions.today: 6 AM is dawning, with ominous clouds fast encroaching on the Hole. But don't be alarmed; it's just an approaching round of snow, coming to re-stoke your soul. So, have no fear; grab your gear and come get weird. Scattered showers will be popping off through the morning in prelude to a steadier powfall this afternoon. Gusty west winds will be cranking in the 10 to 40 mph range, further cooling high temperatures that only reach the teens at the summit and the mid-30s in Teton Village. Everyone knows that eternal youth isn't really found in some kind of fountain. But the jury's still out on the effects of a snow-coated mountain. -Lege
conditions.yesterday: Another 3" of new snow has fallen since the lifts stopped spinning yesterday, fostering a fantastic start to the second week of March. Today's forecast is calling for the chance of light snow showers at elevation especially during the morning hours. The low clouds you're seeing on your drive in should break up my mid morning giving way to mostly sunny skies by the afternoon. Southwest winds gusting up to 40 mph are already in place and we could see ridgetop gusts up to 55 mph by the afternoon. Summit temps will peak at 28 degrees today and the base area will hit 46 degrees. Have a great start to your week! - OH
